summary: Gruber reports on the upcoming collaboration between Rupert Murdoch's News Corp and Apple to launch 'The Daily,' an iPad-exclusive newspaper with a new recurring subscription billing system. The publication would cost 99 cents a week and employ only journalists and a small tech team, representing a lean digital-first news model. Gruber clarifies that Apple's role is not building a central newsstand app but rather adding subscription billing APIs to iOS that any app can use. He speculates on the launch timing, suggesting early December, and notes the possibility of background content delivery. While acknowledging Murdoch's mixed digital track record (MySpace), Gruber finds the initiative genuinely interesting as an experiment in paid tablet journalism.
key_points: Apple is building recurring subscription billing into iOS as a platform feature, not a standalone newsstand app — any developer could use the new APIs
The Daily represents a fundamentally new business model: a newspaper with no print or web edition, built exclusively for tablets at 99 cents per week
News Corp developers already have preliminary documentation on Apple's subscription billing APIs, suggesting deep collaboration
Murdoch's bet rests on the idea that iPad users are more immersed and willing to pay than typical web users, projecting 40 million iPads by end of 2011
Gruber tempers enthusiasm by noting Murdoch's $500 million MySpace purchase as evidence his digital instincts are 'far from infallible'
money_quotes: So the cat — or at least part of the cat — is out of the bag on Apple's upcoming support for recurring subscription billing for content.
Granted, Murdoch is the guy who bought MySpace for over $500 million, so his digital instincts are far from infallible, but at the very least it's an interesting initiative.
It's a new, lean mean business model for daily news.

This is not something like iBooks — there is no central 'iNews' or 'Newsstand' app from Apple. Rather, it's a new subscription billing option for apps — true recurring subscriptions — paid through your iTunes account.

It would be nice if there were also a way for content to be delivered periodically in the background, so that when you launch it in the morning, you don't have to wait for it to download today's news.
Although I sincerely doubt Apple is offering much help with the 'growing number of similar devices' aspect.
key_insight: Apple's upcoming subscription billing APIs represent a platform-level bet that recurring payments through iTunes will unlock viable business models for tablet-native journalism and content apps.
